summary: Joel shares a link to Avi Bryant's technique for optimizing duck-typed method calls, expressing appreciation for the clever approach. He then delivers a dry one-liner noting that Ruby remains slow despite such optimizations.
key_points: Avi Bryant developed a technique to make duck-typed method calls faster
Joel finds the optimization technique noteworthy and clever
Despite clever optimizations, Ruby's overall performance remains poor
key_insight: Clever micro-optimizations for dynamic languages don't solve the fundamental performance problems those languages have.
